title: Coined Cathode Rays
description: Goldstein showed that cathode rays could cast shadows and were emitted perpendicular to the cathode surface, naming them 'cathode rays'.

title: Discovery of Canal Rays
description: Discovered positive anode rays (canal rays) in perforated cathode tubes, proving particles also traveled in the opposite direction.

title: Astrophysical Section Potsdam
description: Appointed to direct the astrophysical and physical sections at the Potsdam Observatory, Potsdam, Germany.

title: Hughes Medal Recipient
description: Awarded the Hughes Medal of the Royal Society in recognition of his experimental investigations on electric discharge in gases.

title: Death
description: Goldstein passed away in Berlin, leaving behind fundamental contributions to atomic structure analysis and mass spectrometry.
